qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled deregistration callback URI enables SSRF from UDM
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding abuses `DeregCallbackUri` in the UECM registration flow and reaches `SendOnDeregistrationNotification`; the advisory patch is for the `/sdm-subscriptions` callback route and the nil-dereference in `DataChangeNotificationProcedure`, which is a different source-to-sink path and root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Event exposure subscriptions can be deleted across tenants by any NUDM_EE caller
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authorization flaw in the NUDM_EE delete/update handlers for `ee-subscriptions`; it does not involve the callback routing bug, malformed `/sdm-subscriptions` path handling, or the `DataChangeNotificationProcedure` nil-pointer dereference described by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable callback code path: the GHSA is a nil-pointer denial of service in `HandleDataChangeNotificationToNF` and `DataChangeNotificationProcedure` for malformed `/sdm-subscriptions` requests, while the session's qualifying findings are a UECM callback-URI SSRF and an Event Exposure IDOR.
evidence: The upstream GHSA patch changes the callback route from `/sdm-subscriptions` to `/:supi/sdm-subscriptions`, validates callback request fields in `internal/sbi/api_httpcallback.go`, and therefore targets the HTTP callback handler rather than UECM or Event Exposure APIs.

The same patch adds a `ue == nil` guard and a `problemDetails == nil` return path in `internal/sbi/processor/notifier.go:DataChangeNotificationProcedure`, confirming the advisory root cause is a nil-pointer dereference in the SDM callback notification loop.

`qualifying_deregcallback_ssrf` instead depends on attacker control of `DeregCallbackUri` in `PUT /nudm-uecm/v1/:ueId/registrations/*` and reaches `SendOnDeregistrationNotification`, which is a different notifier function and different vulnerable operation.

`qualifying_ee_subscription_idor` is confined to `DELETE`/`PATCH /nudm-ee/v1/:ueIdentity/ee-subscriptions/:subscriptionId` and an ownership-check omission in Event Exposure subscription handling, with no overlap with the advisory callback route or nil dereference.
